court: United States District Court, Northern District of California
status_at_anchor: Conditional ADEA collective certification granted May 16, 2025; opt-in period opened January 13, 2026; discovery ongoing

input.title: Mobley v. Workday — AI hiring algorithm age/race discrimination (ADEA + Title VII)
input.description: Plaintiff Derek Mobley alleges Workday's algorithmic candidate-scoring tool — embedded in hiring workflows of hundreds of employer-clients — systematically rejected applications from candidates over 40, Black candidates, and candidates with disabilities. Mobley alleges 100+ rejections from Workday-powered employers. Conditional ADEA collective certified May 2025. Court ruled Workday could be liable as an 'agent' of its employer-customers — landmark vendor-liability question.

events.description: Workday algorithm trained on historical hiring decisions reflecting documented industry bias against older / Black / disabled candidates
Algorithm scores Mobley's resumes; despite credentials, scores consistently below pass-through threshold across employer instances
Disparate-impact scoring observed across protected-class applicants per EEOC analysis
Auto-rejection emails sent to Mobley by employer-clients within minutes/hours of application — pattern consistent with no human review of algo recommendation
Employer HR functions failed to apply independent review of algo recommendation; no human-in-the-loop check on protected-class outcomes
Employer-clients failed audit obligations under EEOC Title VII for AI hiring tools per 2022 guidance
